g.data-narration: Welcome to Android UI! In this lecture, we'll explore Views, ViewGroups, and how to create beautiful interfaces using XML layouts.
The UI is the primary point of interaction between your app and users. A well-designed interface drives user adoption and retention.
Android UI is built on a hierarchical structure. View is the base class, and ViewGroup containers hold other views like a tree.
View is the fundamental building block. Every UI element - TextView, Button, ImageView - extends the View class.
ViewGroup is a special View that acts as a container. It organizes child views using layout managers like LinearLayout or RelativeLayout.
XML layouts allow declarative UI design. You define the interface in XML files, separate from your Kotlin or Java logic code.
g.text: • Easy visual editing in Android Studio

g.data-narration: Key layout attributes include layout_width, layout_height, padding, margin, gravity, and orientation. These control how views are sized and positioned.
Two essential values: match_parent fills the parent's available space, while wrap_content uses only enough space for the content.
Layout inflation converts XML layout files into actual View objects at runtime. This happens when an Activity is created.
Use dp (density-independent pixels) for layouts and sp (scale-independent pixels) for text. This ensures consistent appearance across different screen densities.
Let's analyze the example: LinearLayout organizes children vertically with orientation=vertical. Each child uses wrap_content to size based on content.
Every Android app uses these UI concepts. From simple calculators to complex social media feeds, all interfaces use Views and ViewGroups.
Key takeaways: View is the base class, ViewGroup is the container, XML defines layouts declaratively, and dp/sp ensures consistent sizing across devices.
Practice creating layouts with different ViewGroups, understand match_parent vs wrap_content, and use Android Studio's visual layout editor for hands-on learning.
g.text: Next: Layout Types → Event Handling → Navigation
